docs.title: Industrial Broiler Production Ontology
docs.text: Welcome to the Industrial Broiler Production Ontology (IBPO) documentation.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The IBPO is a comprehensive systems ontology for industrial broiler production that integrates knowledge from: - Biology - avian physiology, genetics, breed characteristics - Engineering - infrastructure, environmental controls, monitoring systems - Veterinary Science - health, diseases, welfare management - Economics - production costs, resource utilization, performance metrics

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Well-defined individuals for production phases (Brooding, Growing, Pre-Processing), genders (Male, Female), and observable characteristics (Weight, Temperature)
  • Comprehensive relationships between broilers, infrastructure, sensors, and observations
  • Strict cardinality constraints ensuring data consistency
  • Deprecated properties maintained for backward compatibility
  • Multi-format releases (OWL, Turtle) for interoperability

docs.text:

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: Thank you for your interest in contributing to the Industrial Broiler Production Ontology!

docs.title: Reporting Issues
docs.text:
Found a bug or have a suggestion?

  1. Check existing issues first
  2. Create a new issue with:
  3. Clear title and description
  4. Steps to reproduce (if applicable)
  5. Expected vs actual behavior

docs.title: For Small Changes
docs.text:
1. Fork the repository
  2. Create a branch: git checkout -b fix/issue-description
  3. Make changes to src/ontology/broiler-production-ontology-edit.ttl
  4. Test locally (see Development)
  5. Commit with clear message: git commit -m "Fix: [description]"
  6. Push and open a Pull Request

docs.title: For Major Changes
docs.text:
1. Open an issue first to discuss the proposed changes
  2. Get feedback from maintainers
  3. Submit a detailed PR with:
  4. Clear description of changes
  5. Motivation and context
  6. Test results

docs.title: Turtle Formatting
docs.text:
# Use consistent indentation (spaces, not tabs)
@prefix : <http://example.org/broiler-ontology#> .

:ClassName a owl:Class ;
  rdfs:label "Class Name" ;
  rdfs:comment "Detailed description of the class." ;
  rdfs:subClassOf :ParentClass .

# Align properties for readability
:property1 rdfs:domain :ClassA ;
           rdfs:range :ClassB ;
           rdfs:comment "Property description." .

docs.title: Naming Conventions
docs.text:
• Classes: CamelCase (e.g., BroilerBreed, SensorObservation)
  • Properties: camelCase (e.g., hasGender, observedAt)
  • Individuals: CamelCase with underscores (e.g., BroodingPhase, Ross308_Assignment_001)

docs.title: Documentation
docs.text:
• Add rdfs:label to all classes and properties (English)
  • Add rdfs:comment with clear, concise descriptions
  • Include usage examples in comments when helpful
:hasGender a owl:ObjectProperty ;
  rdfs:label "has gender" ;
  rdfs:comment "Relates a broiler to its biological sex (Male or Female). Each broiler has exactly one gender." ;
  rdfs:domain :Broiler ;
  rdfs:range :Gender .

docs.title: Testing Your Changes
docs.text:
Before submitting:

  1. Run validation tests 

    cd src/ontology
sh ./run.sh make test

  2. Build release artifacts 

    sh ./run.sh make prepare_release

  3. Check output

  4. Verify no errors in test output
  5. Confirm release files build successfully

  6. Review QC reports in reports/

  7. Test with external tools (optional)

  8. Load into Protégé
  9. Verify with SPARQL queries
  10. Check reasoner output

docs.title: Pull Request Process
docs.text:
1. Update documentation if adding new concepts
  2. Update Ontology Structure

  3. Add examples to Getting Started

  4. Reference issues in your PR description

  5. Link related issues: Fixes #123

  6. Explain the connection

  7. Provide context

  8. Why is this change needed?
  9. What problem does it solve?

  10. How does it align with ontology goals?

  11. Wait for review

  12. Maintainers will review your changes
  13. Be responsive to feedback

  14. Make requested changes in separate commits

  15. Merge

  16. Squash commits if requested
  17. Merge to main
  18. Your change will be included in the next release

docs.title: Documentation Contributions
docs.text:
Help improve documentation!

  • Fix typos and unclear sections
  • Add examples and use cases
  • Improve explanations
  • Translate documentation

Documentation changes: - Edit .md files in docs/ directory - Check formatting in Markdown - Test locally with mkdocs serve (if available)

docs.title: Design Discussions
docs.text:
For major design decisions:

  1. Open a Discussion on GitHub
  2. Explain the proposed change and rationale
  3. Propose multiple solutions if applicable
  4. Gather feedback from the community

docs.title: Development Setup
docs.text: See Development for: - Local environment setup - Building and testing - Common troubleshooting

docs.title: Legal
docs.text: By contributing, you agree that: - Your contributions can be used under the project's license - You have the right to contribute - Your work is original or properly attributed

docs.title: Questions?
docs.text:
• Open a GitHub issue with the question label
  • Check existing discussions
  • Review Ontology Structure

Thank you for helping improve the Broiler Production Ontology! 🎉

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Docker (for ODK build system)
  • Git
  • Turtle/RDF-aware editor (optional: Protégé)

docs.title: Clone the Repository
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/bojet051/broiler-production-ontology.git
cd broiler-production-ontology

docs.title: Run Tests
docs.text:
cd src/ontology
sh ./run.sh make test

This runs: - Reasoning test - ELK reasoner checks logical consistency - SPARQL QC checks - Validates against known anti-patterns - OWL 2 DL Profile validation - Ensures OWL compliance

docs.title: Build Release Artifacts
docs.text:
cd src/ontology
sh ./run.sh make prepare_release

Output files: - broiler-production-ontology.owl - Reasoned OWL format - broiler-production-ontology.ttl - Turtle format - broiler-production-ontology-full.owl - Full with imports - broiler-production-ontology-full.ttl - Full Turtle - reports/ - QC and statistical reports

docs.title: Edit Source File
docs.text:
The editable ontology source is: 

src/ontology/broiler-production-ontology-edit.ttl

docs.title: File Format
docs.text:
• Format: Turtle (RDF/Turtle serialization)
  • Character Encoding: UTF-8
  • Line Endings: Unix (LF)

docs.title: Namespace Prefixes
docs.text:
Always use established prefixes: 

@prefix : <http://example.org/broiler-ontology#> .
@prefix owl: <http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#> .
@prefix rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#> .
@prefix rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#> .
@prefix xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#> .

docs.title: Adding a New Class
docs.text:
:NewClass a owl:Class ;
  rdfs:label "New Class Label" ;
  rdfs:comment "Description of the class purpose and usage." ;
  rdfs:subClassOf :ParentClass .

docs.title: Adding a New Property
docs.text:
:newProperty a owl:ObjectProperty ;
  rdfs:label "new property" ;
  rdfs:comment "Description of the property." ;
  rdfs:domain :DomainClass ;
  rdfs:range :RangeClass ;
  owl:inverseOf :inverseProperty .

docs.title: Adding Restrictions
docs.text:
:Broiler rdfs:subClassOf [
  a owl:Restriction ;
  owl:onProperty :hasGender ;
  owl:cardinality "1"^^xsd:integer
] .

docs.title: SPARQL QC Checks
docs.text:
The following quality checks are automatically applied:

Check Description Consequence owldef-self-reference Classes shouldn't have circular definitions FAIL test iri-range IRIs should not be in range positions Advisory label-with-iri Labels shouldn't contain IRIs Advisory multiple-replaced_by Avoid duplicate replacedBy annotations Warning

docs.title: Common Issues & Fixes
docs.text:
Issue: Test fails on reasoning 

ERROR: Unsatisfiable class detected
Fix: Check for conflicting cardinality constraints or circular subclass relationships

Issue: SPARQL QC violations 

WARN: missing_definition for [class]
Fix: Add rdfs:label and rdfs:comment to all classes/properties

docs.title: Development Workflow
docs.text:
1. Create a feature branch 

    git checkout -b feature/add-new-concept

  2. Edit the ontology 

    # Edit in your preferred editor
nano src/ontology/broiler-production-ontology-edit.ttl

  3. Test locally 

    cd src/ontology
sh ./run.sh make test

  4. Fix any QC violations

  5. Review test output
  6. Update ontology

  7. Rerun tests

  8. Commit changes 

    git add src/ontology/broiler-production-ontology-edit.ttl
git commit -m "Add new concept: [description]"

  9. Build release 

    cd src/ontology
sh ./run.sh make prepare_release

  10. Push and create PR 

    git push origin feature/add-new-concept
# Open PR on GitHub

  11. Merge and release

  12. GitHub Actions automatically runs QC
  13. Upon merge to main, docs are updated
  14. Tag releases on GitHub

docs.title: Docker Not Running
docs.text:
ERROR: Cannot connect to Docker daemon
Solution: Start Docker Desktop, then retry

docs.title: Make Target Not Found
docs.text:
ERROR: No rule to make target 'test'
Solution: Ensure you're in src/ontology/ directory

docs.title: Reasoning Timeout
docs.text:
TIMEOUT: Reasoning did not complete in time
Solution: Reduce reasoning scope or check for circular definitions

docs.title: Updating ODK Version
docs.text:
cd broiler_odk_bootstrap
sh seed-via-docker.sh -c -C broiler-odk.yaml
# Review generated changes
cd ..
git diff
git add -p  # Stage relevant changes
git commit -m "Update ODK to latest version"

docs.title: Custom SPARQL Queries
docs.text:
Add new QC rules in src/sparql/: 

# custom-check.sparql
PREFIX owl: <http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>

SELECT ?entity WHERE {
  # Your SPARQL pattern here
}

docs.title: Extending with Modules
docs.text:
Create modular sub-ontologies: 

# src/ontology/modules/genetics.ttl
@prefix genetics: <http://example.org/broiler-ontology/genetics#> .

:Broiler owl:imports <http://example.org/broiler-ontology/genetics> .

docs.title: Resources
docs.text:
• ODK Documentation
  • OWL 2 Specification
  • SPARQL Query Language
  • Protégé Ontology Editor

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
The ontology is available in multiple formats:

  • OWL Format: broiler-production-ontology.owl, broiler-production-ontology-full.owl
  • Turtle Format: broiler-production-ontology.ttl, broiler-production-ontology-full.ttl

docs.title: Using the Ontology
docs.text:
With Protégé: 1. Download the ontology file (OWL or RDF/Turtle) 2. Open in Protégé 3. Explore classes, properties, and individuals

With Code: 

from rdflib import Graph

# Load the ontology
g = Graph()
g.parse("broiler-production-ontology.ttl", format="turtle")

# Query classes
classes = g.subjects(predicate=RDF.type, object=OWL.Class)
for cls in classes:
    print(cls)

docs.title: Production Phases
docs.text:
• BroodingPhase - Young bird rearing (0-7 days)
  • GrowingPhase - Growth and development (8-40 days)
  • PreProcessing - Final stages before processing

docs.title: Broiler Properties
docs.text: Each broiler has: - hasGender (exactly 1) - Male or Female - hasCurrentPhase (exactly 1) - Current production phase - hasBreedLine (some) - Assigned breed (e.g., Ross308) - hasFeedBatch (some) - Assigned feed rations - hasBroilerObservation (some) - Health and performance measurements

docs.title: Observable Characteristics
docs.text:
• LiveWeightCharacteristic - Body weight measurements
  • TemperatureCharacteristic - Ambient temperature readings
  • Custom characteristics - Extensible for domain-specific measures

docs.title: Creating a Broiler Individual
docs.text:
@prefix : <http://example.org/broiler-ontology#> .

:Broiler_001 a :Broiler ;
  :hasGender :Male ;
  :hasCurrentPhase :GrowingPhase ;
  :hasBreedLine :Ross308_Assignment_001 ;
  :hasFeedBatch :FeedRation_GrowerPhase_001 .

docs.title: Recording an Observation
docs.text:
:Observation_Weight_001 a :SensorObservation ;
  :observedAt :TimeInstant_2024_01_15_10_30 ;
  :observesCharacteristic :LiveWeightCharacteristic ;
  :observedOnBroiler :Broiler_001 ;
  :hasNumericValue "2.5"^^xsd:decimal .

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Explore the Ontology Structure
  • See Development for building and validating changes
  • Review Contributing to help improve the ontology

docs.title: Broiler
docs.text:
The main entity representing an individual bird in industrial production.

Properties: - hasGender (exactly 1) - Reference to Male or Female - hasCurrentPhase (exactly 1) - Current production lifecycle phase - hasBreedLine (some) - Breed assignment individuals - hasFeedBatch (some) - Feed ration assignments - hasBroilerObservation (some) - Observations about this broiler

docs.title: Bird
docs.text: Superclass for all avian entities.

docs.title: BroilerBreed
docs.text: Classification of broiler genetics and characteristics. - Examples: Ross308, Cobb500, Hubbard

docs.title: Gender
docs.text: Biological sex classification. - Individuals: Male, Female

docs.title: ProductionLifecycle
docs.text: Phases in broiler development. - Individuals: BroodingPhase, GrowingPhase, PreProcessing

docs.title: Infrastructure
docs.text: Physical equipment and facilities. - Examples: Brooder, Ventilation System, Feeder

docs.title: Sensor
docs.text: Monitoring devices that record observations. - Examples: Temperature Sensor, Weight Scale

docs.title: Observation
docs.text: Abstract base class for all measurements and observations.

docs.title: SensorObservation
docs.text: Automated measurements from sensors. - Properties: - hasNumericValue (exactly 1) - Measured value - observedAt (some) - TimeInstant - observesCharacteristic (some) - What was measured

docs.title: ObservableCharacteristic
docs.text: Measurable properties and traits. - Examples: LiveWeightCharacteristic, TemperatureCharacteristic

docs.title: TimeInstant
docs.text: Specific point in time for temporal data. - Properties: - inXSDDateTime (exactly 1) - ISO 8601 datetime

docs.title: Broiler Relations
docs.text: Property Domain Range Inverse Comments hasGender Broiler Gender isGenderOf Exactly 1 hasCurrentPhase Broiler ProductionLifecycle isCurrentPhaseOf Exactly 1 hasBreedLine Broiler BroilerBreed isBreedLineOf Some hasFeedBatch Broiler Feed isFeedBatchOf Some hasBroilerObservation Broiler Observation - Some

docs.title: Infrastructure Relations
docs.text: Property Domain Range Inverse Comments controls Controller Infrastructure controlledBy - hasBiosecurityProtocol Infrastructure Biosecurity isBiosecurityProtocolFor - managedBy Infrastructure Manager managesInfrastructure - monitoredBy Infrastructure Sensor monitors -

docs.title: Observation Relations
docs.text: Property Domain Range Comments observesCharacteristic Observation ObservableCharacteristic What was measured observedAt Observation TimeInstant When it was measured observedOnBroiler Observation Broiler Which bird observedInInfrastructure Observation Infrastructure Where measured observedBySensor Observation Sensor How it was measured

docs.title: Deprecated Properties
docs.text:
The following properties are maintained for backward compatibility but should not be used in new models:

  • hasBreed (deprecated) → Use hasBreedLine instead
  • hasFeed (deprecated) → Use hasFeedBatch instead
  • isBreedOf (deprecated) → Use isBreedLineOf instead
  • isInPhase (deprecated) → Use hasCurrentPhase instead

docs.title: Production Phases
docs.text:
• :BroodingPhase - Young bird rearing (0-7 days)
  • :GrowingPhase - Growth and muscle development (8-40 days)
  • :PreProcessing - Final preparation (40-47 days)

docs.title: Genders
docs.text:
• :Male - Male birds
  • :Female - Female birds

docs.title: Observable Characteristics
docs.text:
• :LiveWeightCharacteristic - Body weight
  • :TemperatureCharacteristic - Ambient temperature

docs.title: Individual-Based Modeling
docs.text: Production phases and observable characteristics are modeled as named individuals, not classes. This allows: - Direct reference to specific phases in broiler observations - Consistent terminology across the ontology - Easier data integration

docs.title: Cardinality Constraints
docs.text: Strategic use of cardinality constraints ensures: - Each broiler has exactly 1 current phase - Each broiler has exactly 1 gender - Each observation has exactly 1 numeric value - Each time instant has exactly 1 datetime

docs.title: Inverse Properties
docs.text:
Inverse properties are defined for bidirectional queries: 

# Find all broilers of a given gender
?broiler :hasGender ?gender .
# Same result, inverse direction
?gender :isGenderOf ?broiler .

docs.title: Modular Design
docs.text:
The ontology supports imports for domain-specific modules: - Genetics module - Breed definitions and genetic traits - Health module - Diseases, treatments, preventive protocols - Economics module - Cost factors, productivity metrics

(Currently available as examples; extend as needed)

docs.title: Version Management
docs.text:
• Ontology IRI: http://example.org/broiler-ontology
  • Version IRI: http://example.org/broiler-ontology/1.0
  • Format: OWL 2 DL
  • Source Format: Turtle (RDF/Turtle serialization)

For version history, see Release Notes.

docs.title: Version 1.0 (Current)
docs.text: Release Date: April 15, 2026

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Initial release of the Industrial Broiler Production Ontology, successfully migrated to the Ontology Development Kit (ODK) framework.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• ✅ Core classes for broiler production domain
  • ✅ Comprehensive object and datatype properties
  • ✅ Individual-based modeling for production phases and characteristics
  • ✅ Cardinality constraints for data consistency
  • ✅ Backward-compatible deprecated properties
  • ✅ Multi-format releases (OWL, Turtle)
  • ✅ Automated validation and QC checks
  • ✅ GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline
  • ✅ Comprehensive documentation

docs.title: Classes
docs.text:
• Broiler - Main entity representing individual birds
  • Bird - Superclass for avian entities
  • BroilerBreed - Genetic classifications
  • Gender - Binary sex classification
  • ProductionLifecycle - Production phases
  • Infrastructure - Facilities and equipment
  • Sensor - Monitoring devices
  • Observation - Measurement records
  • SensorObservation - Automated sensor readings
  • ObservableCharacteristic - Measurable properties
  • TimeInstant - Temporal annotations
  • And 8+ domain-specific classes

docs.title: Object Properties
docs.text:
• 30+ properties with inverse relations
  • Cardinality constraints on critical properties
  • Clear domain and range specifications
  • Inverse properties for bidirectional queries

docs.title: Individuals
docs.text:
• BroodingPhase, GrowingPhase, PreProcessing - Production phases
  • Male, Female - Gender classifications
  • LiveWeightCharacteristic, TemperatureCharacteristic - Observable traits

docs.title: Validation
docs.text:
• ✅ OWL 2 DL profile compliance
  • ✅ ELK reasoner consistency check
  • ✅ SPARQL QC checks (4 validation rules)
  • ✅ All tests PASSED with 0 violations

docs.title: Documentation
docs.text:
• Getting Started guide
  • Ontology structure reference
  • Development workflow guide
  • Contributing guidelines
  • This release notes document

docs.title: Known Limitations
docs.text:
• IRI namespace uses example.org (can be updated to production IRI when ready)
  • No module imports configured yet (available for future extension)
  • Genetics, health, and economics modules are design sketches

docs.title: Backward Compatibility
docs.text: Deprecated properties maintained for legacy data support: - hasBreed → Use hasBreedLine - hasFeed → Use hasFeedBatch - isBreedOf → Use isBreedLineOf - isInPhase → Use hasCurrentPhase

docs.title: File Formats
docs.text: Format File Status OWL broiler-production-ontology.owl ✅ Released Turtle broiler-production-ontology.ttl ✅ Released OWL (Full) broiler-production-ontology-full.owl ✅ Released Turtle (Full) broiler-production-ontology-full.ttl ✅ Released

docs.title: Platform Support
docs.text:
• ✅ Protégé 5.x
  • ✅ OWL Tools
  • ✅ RDF/Turtle parsers (rdflib, Jena, etc.)
  • ✅ SPARQL Query Endpoints
  • ✅ Web-based ontology browsers

docs.title: Migration Notes
docs.text:
This release represents the successful migration of the standalone Broiler Production ontology into the ODK framework:

  1. Before: Standalone Turtle file with manual validation
  2. After: ODK-managed project with:
  3. Automated build pipeline
  4. Integrated QC framework
  5. GitHub Actions CI/CD
  6. Professional documentation

See ODK_IMPLEMENTATION_GUIDE.md for detailed migration steps.

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
Planned for future releases:

  • v1.1 - Add genetics module with breed-specific traits
  • v1.2 - Health and disease management module
  • v1.3 - Economics and productivity metrics
  • v2.0 - Extended namespace (purl.obolibrary.org IRI)
  • v2.1 - Full OBO Library compliance

docs.title: How to Cite
docs.text:
If you use this ontology in your research, please cite:

@ontology{IBPO2026,
  title={Industrial Broiler Production Ontology},
  author={bojet051},
  year={2026},
  url={https://github.com/bojet051/broiler-production-ontology}
}
